content.verdict: Cavas Wine Lodge is the honeymoon benchmark of Mendoza — a Relais & Châteaux property of just 18 adobe villas scattered through a 35-acre working Malbec vineyard in Luján de Cuyo, with the snow-capped Andes sitting in the middle distance like a backdrop painted for the occasion. Every villa has its own private plunge pool, rooftop terrace with a log fireplace and wicker daybeds, and a wood-burning stove inside — this is a property deliberately designed for couples to never need to leave, and most guests gladly don't for two or three nights.
content.best_room: Book a standard Villa for honeymoon — all villas already include a private plunge pool and rooftop terrace, which is the irreplaceable asset here. The Grand Villa adds a larger living area and a bathtub positioned for mountain views. Whichever category you choose, request a villa on the far western end of the property for the best unobstructed Andes sightlines at sunset and for the most privacy from the main lodge path.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival at the Vineyard
itinerary_7_nights.description: Private transfer from Mendoza airport (around 45 minutes). Welcome Malbec on your rooftop terrace as the sun sets behind the Andes. Unpack, settle in, and let the villa team light your fireplace before a light dinner in the main lodge — an early night sets the pace.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Estate Walk & Spa
itinerary_7_nights.description: A guided morning walk through the estate's Malbec vines with the winemaker, followed by a private barrel tasting in the cellar. Lunch at the lodge, then a couples' wine-therapy ritual at the spa — grape-seed scrub, Malbec bath, and a long massage. Dinner on the rooftop with a tasting menu paired to estate wines.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Luján de Cuyo Winery Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: The concierge arranges a private day tour of three Luján de Cuyo bodegas — typically Catena Zapata, Achaval-Ferrer, and Viña Cobos. Long lunch at one of the wineries, return by late afternoon for a swim in your plunge pool and a quiet evening in.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Andes High Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Head toward Aconcagua for the day with a private driver — Puente del Inca, the Christ Redeemer pass overlook, and a picnic lunch packed by the hotel. Return in time for a sunset aperitif on the rooftop and dinner at the lodge.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Horseback Ride & Asado
itinerary_7_nights.description: A morning horseback ride through the vineyard and the surrounding poplar-lined country roads, followed by a classic Argentine asado lunch beside the pool. Reserve the afternoon for reading, napping, and the plunge pool. The hotel can arrange a private candle-lit dinner on your rooftop.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Uco Valley Day Trip
itinerary_7_nights.description: A full-day drive south into the Uco Valley for lunch at Bodega Salentein or O. Fournier and a tasting at Andeluna or Zuccardi — 90-minute drive each way, but the scenery and Uco wines justify every minute. Return at dusk for a quiet room-service supper.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: A slow breakfast in bed, final swim in your plunge pool, and a farewell glass of Malbec before private transfer to Mendoza airport — or a drive onward through the Andes passes to Santiago de Chile.

caveats: Mendoza summers (January–February) are hot and dry, and the vineyards are green but hazy; March–May (harvest) and September–November are the most romantic windows with cooler nights and clear Andes views.

Cavas accepts children, but the 18-villa layout and the property's honeymoon reputation mean you will almost always be amid other couples — good news for the ambience, but note the pool and dining spaces are not separated.
The lodge is rural and quiet — there is essentially no nightlife and nothing within walking distance, so factor in the 45-minute drive to central Mendoza if you want a city evening.

faqs.question: Is Cavas Wine Lodge good for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: It is one of the finest honeymoon properties in South America. The private plunge pools, rooftop fireplaces, Andean sunsets, and couples-dominant guest mix create a near-perfect atmosphere. Most couples stay four nights and wish they had booked five.

faqs.question: When is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: Late February to early May is harvest season — golden vines, ripe fruit on the racks, and perfect daytime temperatures. September to November offers snow-dusted Andes and pink almond blossoms. Winter (June–August) is cold at night but the rooftop fires are at their most romantic.

faqs.question: How many days should we stay?
faqs.answer: Four nights is the sweet spot — two full vineyard days, one Uco or Andes excursion, and a pure spa-and-villa day. Three works if you're pairing with Buenos Aires and Patagonia; five makes sense for couples who want total decompression.

faqs.question: Is the food and wine programme included?
faqs.answer: The Relais & Châteaux restaurant and wine tastings are à la carte, though many honeymoon packages bundle a daily tasting menu and one winery tour. Ask reservations for the current Relais & Châteaux honeymoon programme when you book.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into Mendoza El Plumerillo Airport (MDZ), a 2-hour flight from Buenos Aires. The lodge arranges private transfers (around 45 minutes). From Santiago de Chile, the 6–7 hour drive through the Andes is one of the world's great road journeys, weather permitting.

faqs.question: What's the dress code?
faqs.answer: Resort smart-casual — linens and sundresses in the day, jackets optional for dinner. Bring layers for the rooftop terrace after sunset even in summer, and proper walking shoes for the vineyard.

content.email_template:

Dear Cavas Wine Lodge team,

My partner and I are honeymooning in Argentina and would love to stay with you for [X] nights between [DATE] and [DATE].

We are particularly interested in: a Villa on the western side of the estate with the best Andes sunset views.

Could you please advise on:
- Availability and rates for our dates
- Any Relais & Châteaux honeymoon packages
- Private Uco Valley and Luján de Cuyo bodega tour options
- An in-villa couples' wine-therapy ritual at the spa
- Rooftop candle-lit private dinner on one evening

Thank you very much.

[Your names]
